name: Sylas Dakrforge
description:
Silas was born in the state of Barkon, where the main requirement for the birth of a boy was his birth with mana. They were considered the most capable and had great potential. Those who were born without mana were immediately taken from their families and put to death by hanging. Children were usually hanged right in front of the hospital windows so that the girls, giving birth, would pray for the birth of a child with mana. Newborn girls were usually left, since they could give birth to a child with a heavenly gift right away.

Ask: why did such a demographic policy exist in Barkon? The answer is obvious and simple - the state was military and was constantly at war with its neighbors. Its ruler, Tahar III, had a plan to subjugate the entire magical world in order to be the most perfect magician, that is, to know all the most powerful spells. Therefore, the state vitally needed military force, and in huge quantities. Silas was not found to have magic at birth, but since the test for its presence was done only a few weeks after birth, the mother (Cartephia) fled with her baby to the border with another state and hid him with her midwife friend, Grandmother Armaita. The mother had to return to her hometown often, and on one of the trips from the city to the place where Armaita lived, she was caught and executed for hiding the child.

Since the village of Repkhrud, where Grandmother Armaita and the newborn Silas lived, was on the border itself, they did not even look for him there, fearing that the mages of the border state would consider the search as an intention to attack.

When Silas turned 3.5 years old, he showed the first signs of the presence of mana in his small body. Noticing this, Armaita immediately began to teach him control. Despite the fact that Armaita lived as a hermit, in her past, she was one of the most famous magicians of the state and at one time held the post of advisor to the Supreme Mage Barkon.

After 1.5 years, young Silas had already mastered control over his mana to perfection, the amount of which was off the charts even compared to those who were born with it. Then the midwife decided to test him for the type of magic. This was done as follows: a full cauldron of water with mana essence was collected, where the child was placed, and set on fire. As soon as the water began to boil, it was colored in different colors:

Fire - red

Earth - green

Water - bright blue

Air - white (transparent)

Darkness - purple

Light - yellow.

Thus, having performed this ritual, Armaita learned that Silas Darkforge's natural element was darkness. But, in the future, upon growing up and further learning magic and the art itself, the magician can discover in himself the ability to use several natural elements and combine them, creating more powerful, strong spells, but this required a lot of effort and practice.

Time passed, and Silas grew up. Since no one lived in the village except for his grandmother, he had no one to talk to or play with, only a cat, which our hero loved to chase. As a result, he was withdrawn, taciturn and distrustful, which affected his future fate.

By the time Silas was 15, his grandmother Armaita had grown very old and was living out her last crumbs. She taught Silas everything she knew. He reread her entire library 3 times, listened to stories about her life and fate, about this huge world, many times. He knew these stories as if he had lived the life of a midwife.

On the boy's 16th birthday, a new war began with a neighboring state, which bordered the village of Repkhrud - the state of Arned. The village was the main staging area for the attack of Barkon's troops, as a result of which Armaita died in battle, trying to protect her grandson, whom she sent to a neighboring state, convincing the Arned magicians that in the future war, the strength of his pupil would definitely be useful to them.

He spent the next 5 years studying and serving the state. For the valiant fulfillment of all assignments at school a
creator: Markfell
